content.content:
1	{
2	  "tool": "circt",
3	  "type": "pull_request",
4	  "issue_number": 10057,
5	  "issue_url": "https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/10057",
6	  "title": "[ImportVerilog] Handle `$monitor([boh]?|on|off)` statements",
7	  "body": "Implement handling for the `$monitor`, `$monitorb`, `$monitoro`, `$monitorh`, `$monitoron`, and `$monitoroff` system task statements. These are very strange: only the very last monitor task executed is active, across all modules in the design. The monitor will then behave like `$strobe`, printing its message at the end of the simulation time step whenever any of its arguments changes. We don't have proper support for `$strobe` and execution at the end of a time step yet, so we just make `$monitor` behave like a `$display` in an `always_comb` procedure.\r\n\r\nAs a starting point, create two global variables that track which monitor is active and whether monitoring hsa been enabled or disabled globally by `$monitoron` and `$monitoroff`. Then lower each `$monitor` to an assignment to the variable tracking which monitor is active, and implement the printing as a separate `moore.procedure always_comb`.\r\n\r\nThis should cover the semantics closely enough for now. The string formatting part is identical to `$display`.",
8	  "labels": [
9	    "ImportVerilog"
10	  ],
11	  "state": "closed",
12	  "created_at": "2026-03-27",
13	  "closed_at": "2026-03-28",
14	  "code_blocks": [],
15	  "linked_pr": null,
16	  "pr_files": null,
17	  "pr_test_files": null
18	}
19

file.content:
{
  "tool": "circt",
  "type": "pull_request",
  "issue_number": 10057,
  "issue_url": "https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/10057",
  "title": "[ImportVerilog] Handle `$monitor([boh]?|on|off)` statements",
  "body": "Implement handling for the `$monitor`, `$monitorb`, `$monitoro`, `$monitorh`, `$monitoron`, and `$monitoroff` system task statements. These are very strange: only the very last monitor task executed is active, across all modules in the design. The monitor will then behave like `$strobe`, printing its message at the end of the simulation time step whenever any of its arguments changes. We don't have proper support for `$strobe` and execution at the end of a time step yet, so we just make `$monitor` behave like a `$display` in an `always_comb` procedure.\r\n\r\nAs a starting point, create two global variables that track which monitor is active and whether monitoring hsa been enabled or disabled globally by `$monitoron` and `$monitoroff`. Then lower each `$monitor` to an assignment to the variable tracking which monitor is active, and implement the printing as a separate `moore.procedure always_comb`.\r\n\r\nThis should cover the semantics closely enough for now. The string formatting part is identical to `$display`.",
  "labels": [
    "ImportVerilog"
  ],
  "state": "closed",
  "created_at": "2026-03-27",
  "closed_at": "2026-03-28",
  "code_blocks": [],
  "linked_pr": null,
  "pr_files": null,
  "pr_test_files": null
}

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from pathlib import Path
4	import json
5	from typing import Any
6	
7	from differential.backend_list import (
8	    BACKEND_CONFIG_DEFAULT_KEYS,
9	    BACKEND_CONFIG_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS,
10	    BACKEND_REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS,
11	)
12	from differential.result_contract import get_error_decision_table
13	from feature.feature_schema import FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS
14	from generate.testcase_bundle import get_testcase_bundle_contract
15	
16	FEATURE_DATASET_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS = (
17	    "schema_version",
18	    "features",
19	    "feature_index",
20	    "feature_graph",
21	    "source_summary",
22	    "build_info",
23	)
24	
25	FEATURE_INDEX_REQUIRED_KEYS = ("by_id", "by_category", "by_source")
26	FEATURE_GRAPH_ENTRY_KEYS = ("requires", "conflicts", "co_occurs", "rarity_score")
27	SOURCE_SUMMARY_KEYS = ("total_features", "by_source")
28	BUILD_INFO_KEYS = ("built_at", "builder", "source_revision")
29	
30	SOURCE_BUNDLE_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS = (
31	    "source",
32	    "total_features",
33	    "features",
34	    "feature_graph",
35	    "build_info",
36	)
37	
38	
39	def load_json_compatible_mapping(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
40	
41	    try:
42	        data = json.loads(path.read_text())
43	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
44	        raise ValueError(f"{path} must contain JSON-compatible text") from exc
45	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
46	        raise ValueError(f"{path} must decode to an object")
47	    return data
48	
49	
50	def _require_mapping(name: str, value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
51	    if not isinstance(value, dict):
52	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an object")
53	    return value
54	
55	
56	def _require_string_list(name: str, value: Any) -> list[str]:
57	    if not isinstance(value, list) or not all(isinstance(item, str) for item in value):
58	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a list[str]")
59	    return value
60	
61	
62	def validate_feature_dataset(data: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
63	    payload = _require_mapping("feature_dataset", data)
64	    if set(payload) != set(FEATURE_DATASET_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS):
65	        raise ValueError(
66	            "feature_dataset top-level keys must be exactly "
67	            f"{FEATURE_DATASET_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS}"
68	        )
69	    if payload["schema_version"] != 1:
70	        raise ValueError("feature_dataset schema_version must be 1")
71	
72	    features = payload["features"]
73	    if not isinstance(features, list) or not features:
74	        raise ValueError("feature_dataset features must be a non-empty list")
75	    feature_ids: list[str] = []
76	    for index, feature in enumerate(features):
77	        feature_map = _require_mapping(f"features[{index}]", feature)
78	        if set(feature_map) != set(FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS):
79	            raise ValueError(
80	                f"features[{index}] keys must be exactly {FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS}"
81	            )
82	        if not all(
83	            isinstance(feature_map[key], str) for key in FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS[:5]
84	        ):
85	            raise ValueError(f"features[{index}] string fields must be strings")
86	        _require_string_list(f"features[{index}].tags", feature_map["tags"])
87	        _require_mapping(f"features[{index}].metadata", feature_map["metadata"])
88	        feature_ids.append(feature_map["feature_id"])
89	
90	    if len(set(feature_ids)) != len(feature_ids):
91	        raise ValueError("feature_id values must be unique")
92	
93	    feature_index = _require_mapping("feature_index", payload["feature_index"])
94	    if set(feature_index) != set(FEATURE_INDEX_REQUIRED_KEYS):
95	        raise ValueError(
96	            f"feature_index keys must be exactly {FEATURE_INDEX_REQUIRED_KEYS}"
97	        )
98	    by_id = _require_mapping("feature_index.by_id", feature_index["by_id"])
99	    if set(by_id) != set(feature_ids):
100	        raise ValueError("feature_index.by_id keys must match feature IDs")
101	    if not all(isinstance(value, int) for value in by_id.values()):
102	        raise ValueError("feature_index.by_id values must be int indices")
103	    for index_name in ("by_category", "by_source"):
104	        group_index = _require_mapping(
105	            f"feature_index.{index_name}", feature_index[index_name]
106	        )
107	        for group_name, members in group_index.items():
108	            if not isinstance(group_name, str):
109	                raise ValueError(f"feature_index.{index_name} keys must be strings")
110	            _require_string_list(f"feature_index.{index_name}.{group_name}", members)
111	
112	    feature_graph = _require_mapping("feature_graph", payload["feature_graph"])
113	    for feature_id, entry in feature_graph.items():
114	        if feature_id not in feature_ids:
115	            raise ValueError(f"feature_graph contains unknown feature_id {feature_id}")
116	        entry_map = _require_mapping(f"feature_graph.{feature_id}", entry)
117	        if set(entry_map) != set(FEATURE_GRAPH_ENTRY_KEYS):
118	            raise ValueError(
119	                f"feature_graph.{feature_id} keys must be exactly {FEATURE_GRAPH_ENTRY_KEYS}"
120	            )
121	        for key in ("requires", "conflicts", "co_occurs"):
122	            _require_string_list(f"feature_graph.{feature_id}.{key}", entry_map[key])
123	        if not isinstance(entry_map["rarity_score"], (int, float)):
124	            raise ValueError(f"feature_graph.{feature_id}.rarity_score must be numeric")
125	
126	    source_summary = _require_mapping("source_summary", payload["source_summary"])
127	    if set(source_summary) != set(SOURCE_SUMMARY_KEYS):
128	        raise ValueError(f"source_summary keys must be exactly {SOURCE_SUMMARY_KEYS}")
129	    if source_summary["total_features"] != len(features):
130	        raise ValueError("source_summary.total_features must match features length")
131	    by_source = _require_mapping(
132	        "source_summary.by_source", source_summary["by_source"]
133	    )
134	    if not all(
135	        isinstance(key, str) and isinstance(value, int)
136	        for key, value in by_source.items()
137	    ):
138	        raise ValueError("source_summary.by_source must map source names to int counts")
139	
140	    build_info = _require_mapping("build_info", payload["build_info"])
141	    if set(build_info) != set(BUILD_INFO_KEYS):
142	        raise ValueError(f"build_info keys must be exactly {BUILD_INFO_KEYS}")
143	    if not all(isinstance(build_info[key], str) for key in BUILD_INFO_KEYS):
144	        raise ValueError("build_info values must be strings")
145	
146	    return payload
147	
148	
149	def validate_source_bundle(data: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
150	    payload = _require_mapping("source_bundle", data)
151	    if set(payload) != set(SOURCE_BUNDLE_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS):
152	        raise ValueError(
153	            "source_bundle top-level keys must be exactly "
154	            f"{SOURCE_BUNDLE_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS}"
155	        )
156	    if not isinstance(payload["source"], str) or not payload["source"]:
157	        raise ValueError("source_bundle.source must be a non-empty string")
158	
159	    features = payload["features"]
160	    if not isinstance(features, list):
161	        raise ValueError("source_bundle.features must be a list")
162	
163	    if not isinstance(payload["total_features"], int):
164	        raise ValueError("source_bundle.total_features must be an int")
165	    if payload["total_features"] != len(features):
166	        raise ValueError("source_bundle.total_features must match features length")
167	
168	    feature_ids: list[str] = []
169	    for index, feature in enumerate(features):
170	        feature_map = _require_mapping(f"features[{index}]", feature)
171	        if set(feature_map) != set(FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS):
172	            raise ValueError(
173	                f"features[{index}] keys must be exactly {FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS}"
174	            )
175	        if not all(
176	            isinstance(feature_map[key], str) for key in FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS[:5]
177	        ):
178	            raise ValueError(f"features[{index}] string fields must be strings")
179	        _require_string_list(f"features[{index}].tags", feature_map["tags"])
180	        _require_mapping(f"features[{index}].metadata", feature_map["metadata"])
181	        feature_ids.append(feature_map["feature_id"])
182	
183	    if len(set(feature_ids)) != len(feature_ids):
184	        raise ValueError("feature_id values must be unique")
185	
186	    feature_graph = _require_mapping("feature_graph", payload["feature_graph"])
187	    for feature_id, entry in feature_graph.items():
188	        entry_map = _require_mapping(f"feature_graph.{feature_id}", entry)
189	        if set(entry_map) != set(FEATURE_GRAPH_ENTRY_KEYS):
190	            raise ValueError(
191	                f"feature_graph.{feature_id} keys must be exactly "
192	                f"{FEATURE_GRAPH_ENTRY_KEYS}"
193	            )
194	        for key in ("requires", "conflicts", "co_occurs"):
195	            _require_string_list(f"feature_graph.{feature_id}.{key}", entry_map[key])
196	        if not isinstance(entry_map["rarity_score"], (int, float)):
197	            raise ValueError(
198	                f"feature_graph.{feature_id}.rarity_score must be numeric"
199	            )
200	
201	    _require_mapping("build_info", payload["build_info"])
202	
203	    return payload
204	
205	
206	def validate_backend_config(data: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
207	    payload = _require_mapping("backend_config", data)
208	    if set(payload) != set(BACKEND_CONFIG_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS):
209	        raise ValueError(
210	            "backend_config top-level keys must be exactly "
211	            f"{BACKEND_CONFIG_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS}"
212	        )
213	    if payload["schema_version"] != 1:
214	        raise ValueError("backend_config schema_version must be 1")
215	
216	    defaults = _require_mapping("defaults", payload["defaults"])
217	    missing_defaults = [
218	        key for key in BACKEND_CONFIG_DEFAULT_KEYS if key not in defaults
219	    ]
220	    if missing_defaults:
221	        raise ValueError(f"backend defaults missing keys: {missing_defaults}")
222	    if (
223	        not isinstance(defaults["compile_timeout_sec"], int)
224	        or defaults["compile_timeout_sec"] <= 0
225	    ):
226	        raise ValueError("defaults.compile_timeout_sec must be a positive int")
227	    if (
228	        not isinstance(defaults["simulate_timeout_sec"], int)
229	        or defaults["simulate_timeout_sec"] <= 0
230	    ):
231	        raise ValueError("defaults.simulate_timeout_sec must be a positive int")
232	    if not isinstance(defaults["runtime_enabled"], bool):
233	        raise ValueError("defaults.runtime_enabled must be a bool")
234	    if defaults["coverage_mode"] != "edge":
235	        raise ValueError("defaults.coverage_mode must be 'edge'")
236	    if (
237	        not isinstance(defaults["llvm_profdata_binary"], str)
238	        or not defaults["llvm_profdata_binary"].strip()
239	    ):
240	        raise ValueError("defaults.llvm_profdata_binary must be a non-empty string")
241	
242	    backends = _require_mapping("backends", payload["backends"])
243	    if not backends:
244	        raise ValueError("backends must not be empty")
245	    for backend_name, backend_cfg in backends.items():
246	        if not isinstance(backend_name, str):
247	            raise ValueError("backend names must be strings")
248	        if backend_name not in BACKEND_REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS:
249	            raise ValueError(
250	                f"unsupported backend key in backend config: {backend_name}"
251	            )
252	        backend_map = _require_mapping(f"backends.{backend_name}", backend_cfg)
253	        required_keys = BACKEND_REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS[backend_name]
254	        unexpected_keys = sorted(key for key in backend_map if key not in required_keys)
255	        if unexpected_keys:
256	            raise ValueError(
257	                f"backends.{backend_name} contains unsupported keys: {unexpected_keys}"
258	            )
259	        missing_keys = [key for key in required_keys if key not in backend_map]
260	        if missing_keys:
261	            raise ValueError(f"backends.{backend_name} missing keys: {missing_keys}")
262	        for key in required_keys:
263	            if not isinstance(backend_map[key], str) or not backend_map[key].strip():
264	                raise ValueError(
265	                    f"backends.{backend_name}.{key} must be a non-empty string"
266	                )
267	            if not Path(backend_map[key]).is_absolute():
268	                raise ValueError(
269	                    f"backends.{backend_name}.{key} must be an absolute path"
270	                )
271	
272	    return payload
273	
274	
275	def contract_snapshot() -> dict[str, Any]:
276	    return {
277	        "feature_dataset_top_level_keys": list(FEATURE_DATASET_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS),
278	        "backend_config_top_level_keys": list(BACKEND_CONFIG_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS),
279	        "testcase_bundle_contract": get_testcase_bundle_contract(),
280	        "error_decision_table": get_error_decision_table(),
281	    }
282

content.thinking: The contracts.py references `feature.feature_schema.FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS`. I need to read that to know the exact feature schema.

content.content:
1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass
4	from typing import cast
5	from typing import Any
6	
7	FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS = (
8	    "feature_id",
9	    "name",
10	    "category",
11	    "description",
12	    "source",
13	    "tags",
14	    "metadata",
15	)
16	
17	
18	@dataclass(frozen=True)
19	class Feature:
20	    feature_id: str
21	    name: str
22	    category: str
23	    description: str
24	    source: str
25	    tags: list[str]
26	    metadata: dict[str, Any]
27	
28	
29	def _require_mapping(name: str, value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
30	    if not isinstance(value, dict):
31	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an object")
32	    return cast(dict[str, Any], value)
33	
34	
35	def _require_string(name: str, value: Any) -> str:
36	    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
37	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a non-empty string")
38	    return value
39	
40	
41	def _require_string_list(name: str, value: Any) -> list[str]:
42	    if not isinstance(value, list) or not all(isinstance(item, str) for item in value):
43	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a list[str]")
44	    return list(value)
45	
46	
47	def feature_from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> Feature:
48	    payload = _require_mapping("feature", data)
49	    if set(payload) != set(FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS):
50	        raise ValueError(f"feature keys must be exactly {FEATURE_REQUIRED_KEYS}")
51	
52	    metadata = _require_mapping("feature.metadata", payload["metadata"])
53	    return Feature(
54	        feature_id=_require_string("feature.feature_id", payload["feature_id"]),
55	        name=_require_string("feature.name", payload["name"]),
56	        category=_require_string("feature.category", payload["category"]),
57	        description=_require_string("feature.description", payload["description"]),
58	        source=_require_string("feature.source", payload["source"]),
59	        tags=_require_string_list("feature.tags", payload["tags"]),
60	        metadata=dict(metadata),
61	    )
62	
63	
64	def feature_to_dict(feature: Feature) -> dict[str, Any]:
65	    return {
66	        "feature_id": feature.feature_id,
67	        "name": feature.name,
68	        "category": feature.category,
69	        "description": feature.description,
70	        "source": feature.source,
71	        "tags": list(feature.tags),
72	        "metadata": dict(feature.metadata),
73	    }
74

content.thinking:
The feature schema has:
- feature_id
- name
- category
- description
- source
- tags
- metadata

But the user's prompt says: "All features must include: name, category, description, snippet, tags, source_bug_id, tool, issue_url, construct_complexity, ub_type."

Wait, the prompt says the manager should write output containing those fields. But the actual schema required by `validate_feature_dataset` uses `feature_id`, `name`, `category`, `description`, `source`, `tags`, `metadata`. The extra fields (snippet, source_bug_id, tool, issue_url, construct_complexity, ub_type) are probably inside `metadata` or the output format requested by the prompt is slightly different from the final dataset schema.

Looking at the Step 4 example in the system prompt:
```json
{
  "tool": "verilator",
  "issue_number": 1234,
  "title": "...",
  "features": [...],
  "extraction_summary": {
    "code_blocks_found": 2,
    "features_extracted": 5,
    "features_after_dedup": 4
  }
}
```

This is the `features.json` format for a single issue's output, NOT the merged dataset format. So the `features.json` can use the format specified by the prompt, where each feature has `name`, `category`, `description`, `snippet`, `tags`, `source_bug_id`, `tool`, `issue_url`, `construct_complexity`, `ub_type`. The final merged dataset will be validated by `contracts.py`, but that's a later step (run-all.py does that).

Wait, the instructions say: "最终输出 `output/merged_history_v2_features.json` 必须通过 `/edazz/EDAzz/tools/contracts.py:validate_feature_dataset()` 验证." This means the merge script will convert the per-issue `features.json` into the final dataset format. So the per-issue `features.json` format is the one specified by the prompt.

Let me check if there's an example in the repo.

content.thinking:
Great! I can see the exact format. Key observations:
- `construct_complexity` is an integer (1 or 2 in the example)
- `source_bug_id` is just the number "10001"
- `ub_type` can be "undefined" (maybe for crashes/unsupported features) or other values
- Description starts with "Code should include"
- Snippet doesn't need full module wrapper

For this PR about `$monitor`, `$monitorb`, `$monitoro`, `$monitorh`, `$monitoron`, `$monitoroff`:
- This is an ImportVerilog PR adding support for these system tasks.
- The bug/unimplemented feature caused these constructs to fail during import.
- `ub_type`: Since this is about missing support causing likely a compilation/import error, "undefined" might still be appropriate, or maybe "compile_error"? Looking at other examples, let me check another issue.

Let me check a few more examples to see what ub_type values are used.
